title: Tynsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, from Tynsborough, charged with murder after remains found under his garage, believed to be Jill Kloppenburg.
Police uncovered a bag with human remains using radar technology to scan and excavate Sullivan’s garage floor.
Kloppenburg was reported missing in February 2025; remains' identity and cause of death under investigation by Medical Examiner.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman from Houston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her son Charles and his doctor.
Charles suffers from Dup15q syndrome, leading Felterman to seek treatment from Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH.
Felterman aims to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital, highlighting the care her son received there.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his wife Coney's cancer battle.
Coney Flowers, diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024, received treatment, including a bone-marrow transplant.
Ben is raising money for Caring for a Cure, an organization that supported them during Coney's treatment journey.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.
The initiative allows donors to honor someone affected by cancer, expanding the 'I Run For Them' crew.
Labrie's race is inspired by personal stories, including young patients like Maddie, Ashton, and Meg.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' will premiere on Netflix on April 13.
Kahan's documentary covers his life, music, and mental health journey, filmed over a year and a half.
His fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide', will be released on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller resumes his role at WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after layoffs last fall.
His 'Keller At Large' show will return Sunday at 8:30 a.m. featuring Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller will continue writing the MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering a bill by State Sen. John F. Keenan to end the twice-a-year clock change.
83% of Boston.com readers in an informal poll support ending the clock change, but opinions vary on the permanent time system.
Concerns include potential disruption to schedules and health, with proposals needing neighboring states' agreement for implementation.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after a high-speed chase and crash in New Hampshire.
The teen drove a 2014 Chrysler 300 at 118 mph and fled police on I-95 before crashing.
He faces charges including felony reckless conduct and aggravated driving under the influence.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Little Compton, Rhode Island, hosts the annual 89-foot 'World's Shortest St. Paddy's Day Parade'.
The parade featured costumes, music, and New England Patriots Hall of Famer Matt Light as guest of honor.
Ticket sales for the event's corned beef dinner raised over $10,000 for local food banks.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts ER doctors report long wait times due to staffing shortages, limited hospital beds, and gaps in primary care.
Patients in Massachusetts spend an average of 189 minutes in ERs; only Maryland and Delaware have longer waits.
Doctors cite aging population and hospital bed shortages as major factors causing emergency room bottlenecks.
